basis.reasoning:
Assessment reasoning:
- Field description calls for an output under 1800 words, leading with essential allowlist, citing sources, and distinguishing essentials vs demote aspects.
- The provided field_answer describes Hermes Agent default skills, distinguishing bundled (essential) vs optional/demoted integrations (Spotify, Discord, Home Assistant) and includes a demote verdict for those. It also includes a Security/OAuth discussion and a mid-2026 official toolset model, which aligns with the Information of interest.
- Relevance check: The answer covers what constitutes essential allowlist (bundled, always on) and what is demoted (opt-in only), includes a framework for evaluating hobby integrations, and cites multiple sources at the end. It also references OpenClaw/OpenClaw-style defaults for comparison, which matches the Competitor Defaults section.
- Completeness judgment: The field answer directly addresses essential vs demote, discusses OAuth risk when strangers interact, and outlines the official toolset model. It appears to cover all aspects expected by the field description and query, within the word limit. Therefore, completeness = true.
- Excerpt support check: The excerpts provided include notes about bundled vs optional skills, optional-skills catalog, and security considerations, which substantiate the key claims in the field answer. Therefore, supported = true.
- Confidence: Moderate to high given alignment between the answer and excerpts; minor risk about exact phrasing versus excerpts, but core claims are supported.

excerpts:
hermes-agent/website/docs/guides/oauth-over-ssh.md at main ...
This works perfectly when Hermes and your browser are on the same machine. It breaks the moment they aren't: your laptop's browser tries to reach 127.0.0.1 on your laptop, but the listener is bound to 127.0.0.1 on the remote server.
citations.title: hermes-agent/website/docs/guides/oauth-over-ssh.md at main ...

excerpts:
OAuth over SSH / Remote Hosts | Hermes Agent
This works perfectly when Hermes and your browser are on the same machine. It breaks the moment they aren't: your laptop's browser tries to reach 127.0.0.1 on your laptop, but the listener is bound to 127.0.0.1 on the remote server.
citations.title: OAuth over SSH / Remote Hosts | Hermes Agent

excerpts:
OAuth & Token Lifecycle | nousresearch-hermes-agent/hermes ...
Hermes Agent implements a three-tier credential hierarchy for OAuth-based providers. This ensures that long-lived credentials (refresh tokens) are used to generate short-lived session tokens, which are then used to mint specific keys for the inference runtime.
citations.title: OAuth & Token Lifecycle | nousresearch-hermes-agent/hermes ...

excerpts:
## Critical Findings (4)
### C1: Unrestricted shell command execution (local backend)
### C2: Full filesystem read access with no deny list
* Dangerous command detection is regex-based and bypassable.
* The terminal tool passes arbitrary commands to `bash -c` via `subprocess.Popen` . The LLM can execute ANY shell command on the host when `env_type="local"` (the default).
citations.title: Security Audit: 4 Critical, 9 High severity findings in default configuration · Issue #7826 · NousResearch/hermes-agent · GitHub

excerpts:
Configuration — agents | OpenClaw Docs
skills: optional per-agent skill allowlist. If omitted, the agent inherits agents.defaults.skills when set; an explicit list replaces defaults instead of merging, and [] means no skills.
citations.title: Configuration — agents | OpenClaw Docs

excerpts:
Skills Config - OpenClaw
All skills-related configuration lives under skills in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. For built-in image generation/editing, prefer agents.defaults.imageGenerationModel plus the core image_generate tool. skills.entries.* is only for custom or third-party skill workflows.
citations.title: Skills Config - OpenClaw

excerpts:
Skills – OpenClaw - Open Source AI Coding Assistant
OpenClaw uses AgentSkills -compatible skill folders to teach the agent how to use tools. Each skill is a directory containing a SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter and instructions.

excerpts:
openclaw/docs/tools/skills.md at main · openclaw/openclaw
`openclaw skills install` installs into the active workspace `skills/` directory by default. Add `--global` to install into the shared `~/.openclaw/skills` directory, visible to all local agents unless agent allowlists narrow it.
